originalTitle: John Paulson says we are in the early stages of a long-term bull market for gold
summary: He said demand for bullion continues to broaden, led by central banks that have been adding to their reserves alongside growing private-sector interest.
content:
John Paulson, the hedge fund manager who made billions betting against the U.S. housing market before turning bullish on gold, said he believes the precious metal is only in the early stages of a long-term rally.

"I do think we're in the beginnings or the early stages of a long-term bull market for gold," Paulson said on CNBC's "The Exchange" Wednesday. "As people lose faith in paper currencies, gold as an alternative will continue to grow."

Paulson, whose wager against subprime mortgages became one of the most profitable trades in Wall Street history, shifted his focus to gold in 2009, arguing that the unprecedented fiscal and monetary stimulus following the financial crisis would ultimately weaken the U.S. dollar. Since then, gold prices have roughly quadrupled, topping the $5,000 threshold before pulling back.

The billionaire investor said demand for bullion continues to broaden, led by central banks that have been adding to their reserves alongside growing private-sector interest.

"Gold is becoming the most apt reserve currency in the world, replacing fiat currencies," Paulson said. "The demand from central banks, for instance, has continued to grow, as has the private sector."

Paulson also argued that investors stand to benefit more from owning gold miners than bullion itself, particularly companies with large undeveloped reserves.

"I think the greatest way to invest is to invest in early-stage gold stocks," he said.

Paulson made the comments as NovaGold Resources announced it would acquire Paulson Advisers' 40% stake in the Donlin Gold project in Alaska. Paulson, who serves as co-chairman of NovaGold, said the company offers investors leveraged exposure to rising gold prices because of its sizable resource base.

"NovaGold has 40 million ounces of gold indicated and measured resources and reserves at the market [capitalization] of $4.2 billion," Paulson said. "I think the best way to play gold is through stocks like NovaGold, if not NovaGold itself."
chineseTitle: 约翰·保尔森表示，我们正处于黄金长期牛市的早期阶段
chineseSummary: 他表示，在各国央行不断增加储备以及私营部门兴趣不断增长的带动下，对金条的需求持续扩大。
chineseContent:
对冲基金经理约翰·保尔森(John Paulson)在转而看好黄金之前，曾在美国房地产市场上做空数十亿美元，他表示，他认为这种贵金属仅处于长期反弹的早期阶段。

保尔森周三在 CNBC 的《The Exchange》节目中表示：“我确实认为，我们正处于黄金长期牛市的开始或早期阶段。” “随着人们对纸币失去信心，黄金作为替代品将继续增长。”

保尔森对次级抵押贷款的押注成为华尔街历史上最赚钱的交易之一，他在 2009 年将注意力转向黄金，认为金融危机后前所未有的财政和货币刺激措施最终将削弱美元。此后，金价大约上涨了四倍，突破了 5,000 美元的关口，然后又回落。

这位亿万富翁投资者表示，在各国央行不断增加储备以及私营部门兴趣不断增长的带动下，对黄金的需求持续扩大。

保尔森表示：“黄金正在成为世界上最合适的储备货币，取代法定货币。” “例如，中央银行的需求持续增长，私营部门也是如此。”

保尔森还认为，投资者从拥有金矿企业中获得的利益将比持有金条本身更多，尤其是拥有大量未开发储备的公司。

“我认为最好的投资方式是投资早期黄金股票，”他说。

保尔森发表上述言论之际，NovaGold Resources 宣布将收购 Paulson Advisers 所持有的阿拉斯加 Donlin Gold 项目 40% 的股份。 NovaGold 联席董事长保尔森表示，由于该公司拥有庞大的资源基础，因此该公司为投资者提供了金价上涨的杠杆敞口。

保尔森表示：“NovaGold 拥有 4000 万盎司黄金，显示和测量的资源和储量市场[资本]为 42 亿美元。” “我认为投资黄金的最佳方式是通过像 NovaGold 这样的股票，即使不是 NovaGold 本身。”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
